questions.q: Which office did Dr. Rajendra Prasad hold as the first person to do so in independent India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, assuming office in 1950.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. Rajendra Prasad become the first President of India?
questions.explanation: He became President on 26 January 1950, when India became a republic.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. Rajendra Prasad's tenure as President of India end?
questions.explanation: He served as President until 1962, completing the longest tenure in that office.

questions.q: Over which body did Dr. Rajendra Prasad preside before becoming President of India?
questions.explanation: He served as President of the Constituent Assembly that drafted the Constitution.

questions.q: What academic title is commonly associated with Rajendra Prasad's name?
questions.explanation: He is commonly referred to as Dr. Rajendra Prasad.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad holds the record for the longest tenure in which office?
questions.explanation: His twelve-year tenure remains the longest of any President of India.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the constitutional head of state under which form of government established in 1950?
questions.explanation: India became a republic in 1950, with the President as its constitutional head of state.

questions.q: On which national day did Dr. Rajendra Prasad first assume the office of President?
questions.explanation: He took office on 26 January 1950, the first Republic Day.

questions.q: As President, Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the constitutional head of which country?
questions.explanation: He was the head of state of the Republic of India.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad presided over the Constituent Assembly that produced which document?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly he presided over framed the Constitution of India.

questions.q: How many terms did Dr. Rajendra Prasad serve as President of India?
questions.explanation: He was elected to two terms, serving continuously from 1950 to 1962.

questions.q: In which year was Dr. Rajendra Prasad first elected President of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: He was elected President of the Constituent Assembly in December 1946.

questions.q: Who succeeded Dr. Rajendra Prasad as President of India in 1962?
questions.explanation: Dr. S. Radhakrishnan succeeded him as the second President of India in 1962.

questions.q: In the Cabinet of independent India formed in 1947, which portfolio did Rajendra Prasad hold before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served as the first Minister of Food and Agriculture in independent India's interim period before his presidency.

questions.q: Which state was Dr. Rajendra Prasad's home region, associated with his early life and education?
questions.explanation: He was born in the Siwan area of present-day Bihar and is closely associated with that state.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which one?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour, in 1962.

questions.q: By which body is the President of India, the office first held by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, elected?
options: A direct vote of all citizens
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college of members of Parliament and state legislatures.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India throughout Dr. Rajendra Prasad's entire presidency?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was Prime Minister for the whole of Prasad's 1950 to 1962 tenure.

questions.q: What is the standard term of office for the President of India, the post first held by Dr. Rajendra Prasad?
questions.explanation: Under the Constitution the President holds office for a term of five years.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad signed the Constitution into effect in his capacity as the presiding officer of which assembly?
questions.explanation: As President of the Constituent Assembly he presided over the adoption of the Constitution.

questions.q: After becoming a republic, the office held by Dr. Rajendra Prasad replaced which colonial-era office as head of state?
questions.explanation: The office of President replaced that of the Governor-General as head of state in 1950.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad presided over the Constituent Assembly's Drafting Committee chairman, who was which figure?
questions.explanation: B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee while Prasad presided over the Assembly as a whole.

questions.q: In which year did Dr. Rajendra Prasad pass away, shortly after demitting the office of President?
questions.explanation: He died in 1963, the year after leaving the presidency.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as President of the Indian National Congress on more than one occasion. In which decade did he first hold that party post?
questions.explanation: He first served as Congress President in the 1930s, well before independence.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad's birth anniversary falls in which month?
questions.explanation: He was born on 3 December 1884, so his birth anniversary falls in December.

questions.q: Before entering public life, Dr. Rajendra Prasad practised which profession?
questions.explanation: He practised as a lawyer before devoting himself to the national movement.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad's second term as President began in which year?
questions.explanation: He was re-elected and began his second term as President in 1957.

questions.q: Which early Champaran-era movement, in which Rajendra Prasad participated, was led by Mahatma Gandhi in Bihar?
questions.explanation: He was an associate of Gandhi during the Champaran Satyagraha in Bihar in 1917.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly that Dr. Rajendra Prasad presided over held its first sitting in which year?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly held its first sitting on 9 December 1946.

questions.q: As the first occupant of the office, Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President to reside in which building?
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi is the official residence of the President of India.
